programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky is assaulted by a man who claims to have previously undisclosed information about his half-brother, who died decades ago. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The first instalment of a two-part crossover with NCIS: New Orleans sees the Washington, DC team co-operating with their colleagues from the Big Easy to investigate a spate of lethal poisonings on board a New Orleans-to-DC flight. programme.desc: The staff wonder who will benefit from an opportunity for promotion to the board, and Mr Humphries reveals his clairvoyant abilities. Shop-floor comedy, with John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo discovers that an infirm friend needs help to bury his pet in the churchyard, and calls on Alvin and Entwistle to carry out the deed. programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Detective drama, guest starring James McAvoy, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.desc: Gerry and the team agree to help a man who believes his wife is still alive, even though she apparently died 18 months earlier in a car accident. Meanwhile, Brian needs to find a handyman when Esther bans him from his latest DIY project. Hugh Dennis and Samuel West guest star, with Amanda Redman

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team discovers a link between a mummified body and a Notting Hill house that featured as the location for a murderous fight between gangsters in a 1960s film, and present-day renovation work reveals more skeletons behind the walls. Spencer complicates matters further by revealing he lived there with his family when the events must have taken place. Crime drama, starring Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston and Holly Aird

The case against the men grows stronger when it turns out they once before arranged transport for a woman who was attacked, so Emma Keane is sent undercover as a casino punter - only for events to take a surprising turn. Michael Brandon guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol isn't looking forward to April's hen night. David attempts to keep all the women in his life happy - and fails. Ian has big plans for his family's future

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sarah is in trouble, but will Ted find out? What do the police want to chat to Roy about? Phil is annoyed that Kathy asks Ian to be a Godfather without consulting him first

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Colonel Fortune is worried about the prospect of merging with another regiment, and Second Lieutenant Kate Butler faces hostility from the Marines during an exercise

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: When trout farmer Bill Pritchard is accused of murder following his fiancee's mysterious disappearance, Crabbe tries to clear his old friend's name - much to the police's annoyance. Richard Griffiths stars as the corpulent cook, with Alfred Lynch and Maggie Steed

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried decides it is time the practice's image moved with the times. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Lynda Bellingham

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey relishes her role as country commissioner for the Girl Guides when she reprimands Richard for not sponsoring a fundraising event in aid of a new scout hut. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: In recognition of 20 years' service to the store, Captain Peacock is given the key to the executive washroom and a seat in the executive dining room - but the new privileges go to his head. Shop-floor comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo tells Howard to stand up for himself if he still wants Pearl to take him back - but his wife pours cold water on his attempts to impress her. Marina decides to seize the moment with Mr Waddle, and the police pull Tom over for driving too slowly, only to end up sharing his bacon sandwiches. Russ Abbot, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In a bid to persuade Pearl to take back her errant husband, Howard's friends try to convince her he is living as a tramp - but he is far from happy at the prospect of going it alone in the wilderness. Meanwhile, Toby's attempt to regain Monica's affections is thwarted by a dog, and Marina decides to dress down for a change. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The coppers investigate the discovery of a body unearthed during renovations at a new university, and are left puzzling over blackmail, mistaken identity and possible suicide. Feature-length detective drama, with Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan and Prunella Scales

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: An abandoned car belonging to a former Home Secretary is discovered in a Belfast garage, containing a skeleton and an unexploded bomb. Although the garage owner, missing since 1981, may have had links to the IRA, a more disturbing theory soon emerges - that this was the work of British secret service assassins, carefully staged to look like a terrorist outrage. Feature-length episode, guest starring Timothy West, Frances de la Tour and Christopher Strauli

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A woman is robbed of her winnings on the way home from a casino, and suspicion soon falls on the barman who called her a taxi - which was driven by his friend. The case against the men grows stronger when it turns out they once before arranged transport for a woman who was attacked, so Emma Keane is sent undercover as a casino punter - only for events to take a surprising turn. Michael Brandon guest stars

programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.title: Priodas Pum Mil

programme.title: Blwyddyn y Bugail: Ioan Doyle
programme.desc: With lambing season in full swing, Ioan works from early in the morning until late at night, leaving him very little time for climbing. Meanwhile, Helen who has limited farming experience gets a surprise when a ewe gives birth

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a con woman's fake claims of having cancer were exposed by her distinctive wallpaper, and the hidden compartment in a car that brought a gang defrauding charities to justice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create a twist on a classic pudding, rustle up a teatime treat, and use two breakfast favourites to make a new dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a family from Berkshire to find a new home in Devon where the children can enjoy the great outdoors, and visits a farm that grows edible flowers

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Mike and grandson Jan prepare for Petersfield market, hoping to sell their cheeses and home-grown veg alongside Phil and Sue, selling their 100-year-old secret-recipe sausages

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the show in which five dealers bid to buy a retro coat stand, a train set, an unusual chair, some maritime history and a quirky house-clearance find

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Frankie finds Mick, and despite the tension she wishes him a happy birthday, before revealing she is leaving today. Will he stop her getting on a plane to Australia or let her go? With her conscience is pricking at her, Sharon tells Phil she cannot go through with their plan - so he reassures her he will get something stronger to do the job much more quickly. Iqra has exciting news for her family

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar struggles to forgive herself as the hearing reaches a verdict. Jodie's campaign against Beka puts Sacha's life in danger too, and the only hope is that Lucky can work out what is going on before it's too late. While Jac tries to throw herself into her working day, she is unable to shake her concerns about what Kian is getting up to

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When a head-on crash leaves a grandfather dead, Chris discovers the answers could lie in electronic data within the car. As his mission to access it begins, he unearths an intriguing story of what really led up to the incident

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: While Kathy oversees the excavation of Marie's grave, Daniel gives McKinven the lead he needs to make an arrest, but it's the exacting forensic science that finally brings peace for Emma

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Simon receives desperate news from Georgia, and makes yet another attempt to get David and Michael to work together. However, it ultimately falls to a final pair of new potential cast members to offer the pair a new perspective on their friendship before it is too late

programme.title: BAMOUS
programme.desc: Dane Baptiste hosts a topical comedy show exploring the NASBLAQ - a mythical stock index for black performers - through stand-up routines and sketches. Featuring appearances by a exciting up-and-coming comedians

programme.title: Supercar, Superfam
programme.desc: One-off special catching up with Kash and Shabs in the wake of a disastrous fire, as they make plans to restore the custom supercar business bigger and better than before. Kash is unaware that Sheila is making big plans for their wedding anniversary, and a new client presents the team with a challenge that stretches their skills to the limit

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: A report on the scammers preying on those left most vulnerable by the Covid-19 pandemic. Plus, the team of experts offer advice on everything from money worries to medical concerns

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom flat with rental potential in Catford, a terraced house in Dover, and a quirky terraced house in Accrington

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with the Cleary family seven years after they appeared on the show to see if they decided to leave the UK behind for a new life in Australia

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: CCTV footage from a petrol station helps convict a gang of cold-blooded killers. Elsewhere, a criminal's mobile phone finally uncovers a gang targeting the vulnerable

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Detling, Kent, hoping to profit at an auction in nearby Cranbrook. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross offers viewers a few top tips when making their first auction purchase

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown must race to save Bunty from the hangman's noose when she is tried for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams and Emer Kenny

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers update old favourite the Rum Baba, and make the most of a great cut of beef when they create a traditional Greek Stifado

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in the Shropshire countryside with a sociable couple who have £425,000 to invest in a home that gives them space for entertaining and their beloved jukebox

programme.title: President Biden: The Inauguration
programme.desc: A live report from Washington, DC, as Joe Biden is sworn in as the 46th President of the United States, and Kamala Harris takes the oath of office as the new Vice President

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items. This time, seller Pam arrives with a vintage pommel horse, typical of ones that used to be a feature of all school gyms, before Lorraine presents a mid-20th century French wedding globe. Later, Gary hopes to impress the dealers with his vintage table hockey game

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Dominic Chinea undertakes the challenging restoration of a Carrom board, a traditional Asian board game, while Steven Kember teams up with silversmith Brenton West to repair a precious silver menorah. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher restores a small wooden duck once used in magic tricks, and bookbinder Christopher Shaw repairs a pair of photo albums charting Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Fulham v Manchester United at Craven Cottage and Manchester City v Aston Villa at Etihad Stadium, as both Manchester clubs looked for victories to aid their title challenges. Plus, Leeds United v Southampton, West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion and Leicester City v Chelsea

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel Esports enter their second week of the season, and the players start to miss home. YoungBuck feels there is a stigma in the gaming community for those who have a girlfriend

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate lockdown issues, including why some people have had trouble getting refunds for cancelled concerts

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell follow the progress of renovation projects in Stonebroom in Derbyshire, Pontypridd in Wales and Cobridge in Staffordshire

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Lee and Abigail Parsons, who hoped a new life in New Zealand would mean an escape from their hectic lives in the UK

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a simple business card revealed the true identities of a gang of con artists, and a ripped-up note that held the key to locking away two murderers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Edinburgh, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visiting the city's famous underground vaults

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prove that home-made can beat shop-bought when they prepare gravlax, jam doughnuts and their own corned beef

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a Suffolk house hunter find a low-maintenance rural home with a wildlife-friendly garden for £490,000. Plus, a look at traditional boatbuilding skills

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: At one of the UK's oldest and biggest livestock markets, in the town of Melton Mowbray, sheep farmer Elizabeth - with her partner Mitchel - will be hoping to impress her father by selling four of their breeding rams. Meanwhile, experienced mum and daughter Sheila and Julie intend to cement their national reputation for sheep breeding by selling theirs. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on a plate with a famous name on it, a light from an operating theatre, a puzzle box and some Chinese silver - as well as a quirky orange item

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Determined to go through with her plan, Sharon tells Ian she is making a special dinner. He seems wary - until she tells him they should finally consummate their marriage. Jay apologises to Honey for the hurtful things he said to Billy about their kiss. Iqra is disgusted by Ash's betrayal and ends their relationship, and with nowhere else to go, Ash is forced to go home to her family

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as four more professional dog groomers are put to the test. First up in the Breed Makeover round are four English cocker spaniels, as the contestants try to tame the gun dogs' wig and furnishings. They are then challenged to create a tricky spiral around the coats of four frizzy-haired models, before parading them on the dogwalk, where their owners see the results for the first time. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It seems money doesn't buy happiness when lottery winner Cherry is found dead at her luxurious villa, much to the horror of her husband and their visiting friends. As usual, Neville and the team are presented with a real head-scratcher when it turns out no one was in the house at the time - and the mystery deepens when the victim's body apparently vanishes into thin air. Meanwhile, JP is training unruly new officer Marlon, aware that his wife Rosey is due to give birth at any time. Kelvin Fletcher, Jason Manford and Faye McKeever guest star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Jeff and daughters Tracy and Karen take their honey to Bowhouse Farmers' Market in Fife, where organic vegetable growers Connie and Tom also try to sell their produce

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Hill farmer Joyce Campbell prepares spring, and does everything she can to make sure her 800 North Country Cheviot ewes are healthy ahead of birthing season. Meanwhile in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are preparing for the first cattle TB test on their new farm, and in Aberdeenshire, spring has come early for new entrant farmers Isla and George at Little Rowater Farm

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough explores animal oddities, looking at two different creatures with curiously distinctive evolutionary quirks, linked together by a common theme. He examines how hedgehogs and rhinos have developed protective skins, and looks at animals that rely on other species, from cuckoos planting their eggs in other birds' nests to the Death's-head hawkmoth stealing honey

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Katie Thistleton presents Study Tips Tuesday with the help of Youtuber Jack Edwards

programme.title: Magic Numbers: Hannah Fry's Mysterious World of Maths
programme.desc: Dr Hannah Fry travels down the fastest zip-wire in the world to learn more about Newton's ideas on gravity and discusses why maths is so good at predicting the world around us

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the mixed pairs final and a second-round open singles match. Nick Brett and Marion Purcell defeated Greg Harlow and Alison Merrien in last year's mixed pairs showpiece contest

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Mark Stacey finds a horse ledger that is a runaway success and Caroline Hawley uncovers a set of letters from Sir Arthur Conan Doyle

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A family of five works in a corner shop to reveal how such businesses have changed over the course of the past 100 years, aided by presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell. The first episode covers shops from the Victorian era to the First World War. The family must learn to bake their own counter goods, measure loose products like tea, flour and sugar by hand, and make deliveries with a horse and cart. They also receive a visit from Kelly Holmes, who puts them through an Edwardian workout and discover the impact of food shortages on corner shops as the war drew on

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Winter wildlife stories from around the UK. Chris Packham will be in the New Forest watching winter transform his home, and Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some murmurations. Young naturalist Indy Green will be taking us deep into Sherwood Forest on the hunt for one of our most elusive birds of prey - the goshawk

programme.title: Silenced: The Hidden Story of Disabled Britain
programme.desc: Cerrie Burnell presents a history of disabled people's struggle for human rights in Britain. The film looks back to the policies in place in Victorian workhouses, uncovering the original records of an institution created to segregate disabled people and stop them from having children. She also shares inspiring stories of pioneering campaigners for social change, and looks at the challenges still to be faced in the future

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the influence of Wales' different performance traditions on the nation's comedy and on its biggest stars. He discovers the lasting legacy of the popular 70s double act Ryan and Ronnie on some of today's biggest performers, finds out how the folk club scene helped produce Max Boyce and sees how Rhod Gilbert has inspired a new generation of performers - including the latest winner of the Edinburgh comedy award Jordan Brookes

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Tim Key performs his isolation poetry, Morgana Robinson showcases her impersonation of Cheryl Cole and Charlie Higson brings The Fast Show's Swiss Toni back to life. Plus, Bob Mortimer shows what it is possible to create from a slab of butter, a tin of sardines and an orange

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs ends in Finland, where she discovers that climate change has had a serious impact on many traditional trades. In the south of the country, she explores the timber trade by meeting a forest cleaner and a baker. Venturing north, where temperatures are rising the most, she gets to grips with the tourist industry by training as an ice carver and an elf in Santa's village

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, including the mixed pairs final

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Monty Don and his team of horticultural experts feature in this selection of highlights from the 2020 series

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.desc: Property series catching up with UK families after their week in Australia or New Zealand to discover whether they decided to make the move and live there permanently

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Donna Mullings and Ahmed Mudawi meet members of the deaf community to learn about their experiences of racism and how it can be dismantled. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a layered chocolate and peanut butter cake, and uses liquorice pellets to make a sauce for a Basque burnt cheesecake. She also demonstrates a recipe for roast vegetables with cumin and fennel seeds to accompany spiced bulgur wheat and beetroot sauce

programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: With Katie Thistleton

programme.title: Magic Numbers: Hannah Fry's Mysterious World of Maths
programme.desc: As her journey examining whether maths was invented or discovered concludes, Mathematician Dr Hannah Fry explores a paradox at the heart of modern maths, and what maths can reveal about the fundamental building blocks of the universe

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring a ladies' singles semi-final and a second-round open singles match. Eventual ladies' champion Julie Forrest defeated Alison Merrien at this stage last year

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns get a taste of running a store in the 1920s, 30s and 40s, considered the golden age of the corner shop due to the plentiful availability of packaged food. However, rationing quickly has a devastating impact on both the shop and the family's day-to-day lives, with carrot lollies and mutton bacon proving less than enticing meals. The Arderns learn how to make ice cream by hand and blend tea, as well as taking delivery of a vintage fruit machine. They also find themselves at the centre of a community party recreating the VE Day celebrations

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham catches up on the action from the live cameras in the New Forest and on the River Ness in Scotland, while Iolo Williams follows a feral flock of barnacle geese. Elsewhere, cameras delve below the surface of Loch Carron to see the amazing brittlestars who thrive there in winter

programme.title: Targeted: The Truth about Disability Hate Crime
programme.desc: Disabled filmmaker Richard Butchins' brutally honest film explores the abuse and aggression faced by disabled people in everyday life, from verbal insults to violent physical attacks. The film features a wide range of testimony from survivors themselves as they share their own experiences of the abuse they have faced

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A nostalgic look back at the early part of the decade as the music show embraced the advent of 'new pop', by changing its theme tune and titles and parting company with dance act Legs & Co. Acts featured include Duran Duran, Depeche Mode, the Human League, Kim Wilde, Midge Ure, Jerry Dammers (of the Specials), Beggar and Co, Leee John and Freeez. With Richard Skinner, Mike Read and Carrie Grant

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Silenced: The Hidden Story of Disabled Britain
programme.desc: Cerrie Burnell presents a history of disabled people's struggle for human rights in Britain. The film looks back to the policies in place in Victorian workhouses, uncovering the original records of an institution created to segregate disabled people and stop them from having children. She also shares inspiring stories of pioneering campaigners for social change, and looks at the challenges still to be faced in the future

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, including the ladies' singles semi-finals and the second round of the open singles

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs ends in Finland, where she discovers that climate change has had a serious impact on many traditional trades. In the south of the country, she explores the timber trade by meeting a forest cleaner and a baker. Venturing north, where temperatures are rising the most, she gets to grips with the tourist industry by training as an ice carver and an elf in Santa's village

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Donna Mullings and Ahmed Mudawi meet members of the deaf community to learn about their experiences of racism and how it can be dismantled. With voiceover

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A scribbled name in a discarded notebook is the key to putting a dangerous driver behind bars, while the serial numbers on stolen cash cracks the mystery of a dangerous gang

programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles look at Chinese ceramics with an eye-watering price tag

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Dog fanatic Sheridan Smith and her fluffy co-host Stanley are on a mission to find the nation's best professional dog groomer. Heat one sees the first four contestants working on a long-haired shih-tzu - one of the most popular breeds to grace British salons. After that they let their imaginations fly as they give four curly haired canines a teddy bear trim to parade on the dogwalk in front of their owners

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Cel Spellman presents Thinking Thursday, featuring help with cyberbullying

programme.title: The Joy of Data with Dr Hannah Fry
programme.desc: The mathematician explores the world of data, revealing exactly what it is and how it is captured, stored, shared and made sense of. She tells the story of the engineers of the data age, who brought about a technological and philosophical revolution, and confesses that, for her, the joy of data is all about spotting patterns

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the ladies' singles final and a second-round open singles match. Julie Forrest retained the ladies' title last year with a victory over Janice Gower

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire, where antiques expert Jonathan Pratt finds a bronze statue and Catherine Southon enthuses about a silver bowl. Paul also finds out about the life of poet Philip Larkin

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penrith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwall's oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The classicist returns with the cultural review show, and hot on the heels of Joe Biden's inauguration, she talks to Veep and The Thick of It creator Armando Iannucci to ask how comedians and satirists adjust to a new regime. She is also joined by David Olusoga, who recently interviewed Barack Obama, to explore succession through the ages

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson celebrates the food she loves to cook for friends and family at home, demonstrating a wide range of recipes that are warming, comforting and inspirational. She shares new discoveries like her Turkish-inspired eggs, old-fashioned favourites including the aptly named Queen of Puddings, as well as her emergency brownies. Nigella also rustles up a simple chicken and pea tray-bake for a relaxed get-together around the table, and she finds a new use for a spiraliser

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Reports on the wonderful wildlife and seasonal beauty to be found in winter. Chris Packham is in the New Forest looking at the shape of nests and different species of bee, Iolo Williams looks at tree creepers and Gillian Burke reports from the Beaver Project in Cornwall. Cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones tells the story of the Oare Marshes on the River Swale, which has gone from a site making gunpowder to a wildlife haven

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks a completely random selection of questions this week, with Bill Bailey, Daliso Chaponda, Sally Phillips and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Angela Barnes, Catherine Bohart, Ed Gamble, Rhys James and Michael Odewale join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Gina Yashere comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. She warms up the audience before introducing routines by 2018 Britain's Got Talent winner Lost Voice Guy and Mock the Week regular Ed Gamble

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: Targeted: The Truth about Disability Hate Crime
programme.desc: Disabled filmmaker Richard Butchins' brutally honest film explores the abuse and aggression faced by disabled people in everyday life, from verbal insults to violent physical attacks. The film features a wide range of testimony from survivors themselves as they share their own experiences of the abuse they have faced

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, including the ladies' singles final

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Solent, one the busiest stretches of water in the UK, reports come in of a missing kite surfer. The crew of Lymington lifeboat station start searching, but as the hours pass they begin to fear the worst. Twenty miles to the west, the Poole crew are called out to save a father and daughter from drowning as they are swept out to sea, and at Chiswick, one of the few RNLI stations with a crew on permanent standby 24/7, an unusual casualty in the Thames leaves the crew attempting to save not just one but nine lives

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The documentary charting the transformation of celebrity examines how people capitalised on the digital revolution in order to sidestep the traditional routes to fame. When the old tabloid machinery began to lose power, celebrities made the move to the digital press - and soon after pop acts were making a buzz on the internet. Meanwhile, structured reality shows dominated the younger demographic - and all of these things came together through social media. All of a sudden there was a new way of becoming famous

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack's journey takes him to the North East, where he visits a pumping station that ran for 100 years before ceasing operation in 1967 and travels across the Middlesbrough transporter bridge. He also discovers a company in Bradford which still makes boilers in the old-fashioned way, with rivets instead of welding

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a cold, snow-blanketed wintertime landscape scene

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Examining how Barack Obama set out to end George Bush's wars in the Middle East and reset relations with the rest of the world. In Syria, when evidence shows the use of chemical weapons, Obama decides to bomb. But when the British Parliament votes against the intervention, he decides he needs the backing of a reluctant Congress. Foreign Secretary William Hague explains why the British Parliament voted against intervention in 2013. This episode also explores how Obama scored a big win when he negotiated a secret deal to end the nuclear threat from Iran - behind the backs of his closest allies

programme.title: 'Til Kingdom Come: Trump, Faith and Money
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the close ties between US evangelicals and Israel's foremost philanthropic organisation, and its influence on both nations' foreign policies

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Journalist and author Jonathan Freedland examines the US presidency and explores what qualities make a great American leader. He is joined by contributors including James Naughtie, Shirley Williams, Harold Evans, Douglas Hurd, Simon Hoggart and Bonnie Greer to discuss what can be learnt from presidents such as Theodore Roosevelt, John F Kennedy, Richard Nixon and Jimmy Carter

programme.title: Barack Obama Talks to David Olusoga
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga is joined in conversation by former American President Barack Obama to discuss his long-awaited memoir, A Promised Land. He also reflects on the volatile racial divide in the US, his steadfast refusal to abandon American ideals, and revealing details of life as a black First Family in the White House

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Josephine has been blackmailed by the traffickers into defending their smuggler, while Ali and Laure follow whatever leads they can tease out of the new line of enquiry, but how much information can they get out of their new arrest? Captain Beckriche and judge Lucie Bordieu must find common ground to prove a link between the international smuggling operation and the murder case. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure and Ali identify 'the Alsatian' and get to work on a tracking device that was hidden under his car by members of a rival gang. Josephine tries to help Souleymane, but her attempts are inadequate. In French

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a cold, snow-blanketed wintertime landscape scene

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack's journey takes him to the North East, where he visits a pumping station that ran for 100 years before ceasing operation in 1967 and travels across the Middlesbrough transporter bridge. He also discovers a company in Bradford which still makes boilers in the old-fashioned way, with rivets instead of welding

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Examining how Barack Obama set out to end George Bush's wars in the Middle East and reset relations with the rest of the world. Foreign Secretary William Hague explains why the British Parliament voted against intervention in 2013. This episode also explores how Obama scored a big win when he negotiated a secret deal to end the nuclear threat from Iran - behind the backs of his closest allies

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack fulfils a personal ambition by driving his traction engine across the Scammonden Bridge near Huddersfield, West Yorkshire. He also visits a forge in Sheffield and watches crucible steel being made by the experts at Abbeydale Industrial Hamlet

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a warm, elegant snow-kissed scene, with a little cabin snuggled deeply in the embrace of foothills, bushes and evergreen trees

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: The second episode focuses on the monarch's relationship with her daughters after the death of Prince Albert, exploring how Victoria clung to and bullied them, as well as arranging who they should marry. The princesses' response was to fight back, making them unlikely champions of female independence

programme.title: Victoria & Abdul (2017)
programme.desc: Adbul Karim is whisked from his clerk's job to present a ceremonial coin to Queen Victoria for her golden jubilee. The monarch, who is tired of her fawning courtiers, takes a liking to the young man and promotes him to become her Munshi, or spiritual teacher - much to the resentment of her family and household. Period drama, starring Judi Dench, Ali Fazal, Tim Pigott-Smith and Eddie Izzard

programme.title: Judi Dench: All the World's Her Stage
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the life and times of the veteran actress, looking back at the high points of her 60-year career. Billy Connolly recalls the time, when filming Mrs Brown, he thought she was falling in love with him, while Samantha Bond remembers how she calmed Pierce Brosnan's nerves to prepare him for his first scene with the actress. Other contributors include Daniel Craig, Ann Mitchell, Sam Mendes and Ian McKellen. Narrated by Colin Salmon

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott look back at some of the biggest stories of 2020, from the launch of Solar Orbiter to the discovery of phosphine on Venus

programme.title: Night on Film - An A-Z of the Dark
programme.desc: An alphabetical compilation of footage about nocturnal life, featuring everything from bats to vampires as the night comes alive in this unusual mixture of music and archive

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a warm, elegant snow-kissed scene, with a little cabin snuggled deeply in the embrace of foothills, bushes and evergreen trees

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack fulfils a personal ambition by driving his traction engine across the Scammonden Bridge near Huddersfield, West Yorkshire. He also visits a forge in Sheffield and watches crucible steel being made by the experts at Abbeydale Industrial Hamlet

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: The second episode focuses on the monarch's relationship with her daughters after the death of Prince Albert, exploring how Victoria clung to and bullied them, as well as arranging who they should marry. programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack investigates traditional craftsmanship in Yorkshire and Derbyshire, including glass-cutting, making rivets using the traditional hot forge method, and ornate carving. He also catches up with some old friends in the process of restoring a showman's engine and watches workers at the Midland Railway Centre as they repair damaged trains

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a snow-covered forest, highlighted by winter's rustic touches of colour to add a bit of warmth and beauty

programme.title: The Bermuda Triangle: Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Bruce Denardo explores the legend of the region where many planes and ships have disappeared in mysterious circumstances. Using a state-of-the-art submarine, Graham Hawkes reveals how five wrecks ended up 730ft down in the heart of the deadly expanse of water

programme.title: All the President's Men (1976)
programme.desc: The true story of Washington Post reporters Bob Woodward and Carl Bernstein, who persevered in their investigation of the links between the Watergate break-in and the White House, a scandal that eventually forced the resignation of President Nixon. Alan J Pakula's drama, starring Robert Redford, Dustin Hoffman, Jason Robards and Hal Holbrook

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions within a genre that mixes film and music and encompasses many forms, from drama and comedy to fantasy and documentary. There is the classic pop star vehicle, where the biggest acts play themselves, or a version of themselves, as well as dramatised accounts of the stars' lives that dazzle with musical set pieces, while playing on the fascination with the darker side of fame

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Speaking from Mission Control, Robert the Robot instructs Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers to set a course for the giant planet, Jupiter. Excited for the next stage of their journey, Chris notices that the plant pots on top of the Space Sailor are a model of the Solar System and realises that there is a lot of space between the asteroid belt and Jupiter. Maggie explains that Jupiter is too far away for humans to visit so spacecrafts like Voyager make the journey for us. Robert receives an incoming message from Astronomer Edward, who uploads some mission data of Jupiter for Stargazers everywhere to find out how many of Jupiter’s moons they can spot. Back on the Space Sailor, Chris and Maggie encounter their first canal lock and discover that it’s going to take a while longer to get to Jupiter. More Stargazers send an update back to Mission Contro

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she discovers that astronauts live in space on a spacecraft called the International Space Station and that there have been different astronauts living there for over 20 years. Maddie shows us how astronauts live and work on the International Space Station and do lots of different jobs and research in order to learn more about space and our planet Earth. We explore a laboratory where they do experiments, the food they eat, how they wash and even how they go to the toilet. We find out that the space station is made from thousands of parts that were put together by astronauts in space, and we learn that it’s also a home because it’s a place where astronauts eat, sleep and have fun.

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Robert the Robot instructs Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers to set a course for the next planet on their journey, Saturn. Before setting off, Maggie explains how excited people were when Voyager sent back the first pictures of Saturn and its rings. In Mission Control, Robert receives another incoming message from Astronomer Edward who uploads some mission data of Saturn and asks Stargazers everywhere to try and spot its rings. Back on Space Sailor, Chris and Maggie are still on their way to Saturn. Maggie explains that it took Voyager over three years to get there but points out that there are lots of sights to enjoy while they make their journey, like the sunlight lighting up the canal. More Stargazers send an update back to Mission Control to inform Robert that the Sun’s light also lights up Saturn’s icy rings. Finally reaching their destinati

programme.desc: Mister Maker receives a call from his friend Cat Sandion, who needs help with a puppet show. Mister Maker shows Cat how to make the perfect puppet out of an old paper bag. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister M makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art. This leads to another arty idea where a terrific Triangle is created from lolly sticks, paper, paint and glue. Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.desc: In the first episode we meet four-year-old Ottalie who lives on a farm. Today Ottalie helps out, collecting fresh eggs from the chickens, cleaning out their run, and giving them new straw. Back inside it's time for a game of hide-and-seek with Mum and little sister Imogen. But when Dad gets home, will he give away Mum's hiding place?

programme.title: Swashbuckle

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: The presenter travels 150 million years into the past to the time of the plant-eating brachiosaurus, and also tries to find a branch of a monkey puzzle tree

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how the robots astronauts use in space have helped us to create robots we use here on Earth. We discover there are lots of different types of space robots, robonauts that help astronauts to do jobs, space probes that travel to far off parts of the solar system, and landers and rovers - robots that land on planets like Mars to collect information. We learn how robots are sent on special missions to Mars and how they take pictures and send information back to Earth. Maddie visits a research centre to meet a Mars rover robot and uses her special cameras to help us see how it moves over the ground.

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Robert the Robot tells Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers that the next stop on their journey is Uranus. Maggie explains that it took Voyager 2 five years to make the journey from Saturn to Uranus – it’s nearly three billion kilometres away from earth! In Mission Control, Robert receives another incoming message from Astronomer Edward, who uploads some pictures of Uranus from his telescope for Stargazers everywhere to look at. Back on the Space Sailor, Chris is amazed at how far Uranus is from Earth. Maggie explains that it could’ve been a lot further if it weren’t for the planets’ alignments being just right for Voyager 2’s mission. More Stargazers send an update back to Mission Control to inform Robert that like Saturn, Uranus also has rings! Keen to see the rings up close, Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers arrive at Uranus and discover another S

programme.desc: Donald Trump has secured his unique place in the history books, the first President in American history to be impeached twice. What that means in practical terms isn't clear. There'll be no Trump trial in the Senate before Joe Biden moves into the White House but Democrats insist he will be held to account for the assault on the Capitol. Stephen Sackur speaks to the veteran lawyer Alan Dershowitz, part of the Trump defence team in impeachment 1. Will he get involved in the sequel and how will it play in a divided America?

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Crewe sort the treasures from their trash, including a cheeky chimp, a collection of autographs, and some modern art

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which three pairs of contestants use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Kimberley, James, Niall and Nicola, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paul breaks down and makes a big admission. Tracy is jealous. Kim enlists Mack's help in a plan

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter flies into Upington, the gateway to the Kalahari, and follows the Orange River to the spectacular Augrabies Falls. Paddling down river, Gregg stays overnight at Khamkhirri Lodge and has a traditional Afrikaans barbecue. He then heads into the heart of the desert where he faces a tough taste challenge and is surprised by a secret spring

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor travels to the Tiwi Islands, swims with a whale shark, and visits the Houtman Abrolhos, a string of over 100 little islands. Martin Clunes also samples life on Rottnest Island, which has long been a playground retreat for mainland visitors

programme.title: Outbreak: The Virus That Shook the World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the spread of Covid-19 across four continents during 2020. Featuring contributions from medical professionals, a look at the initial outbreak in China and suggestions that the authorities attempted to cover up the danger, and an examination of how the decisions taken by governments and public health officials helped shape the course of the infection

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams narrates a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: As the makeover show returns, Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Fareham near Southampton to surprise Betty Richards, a life-long volunteer. The 90 year old suffers from macular degeneration, and her garden is a green-free concrete box - with so many levels and trip hazards that it's off limits to the sight-impaired dare-devil fundraiser, so the team sets about transforming it into a safe haven

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi strikes lucky while digging for dirt on Ray, Sean and Kirk call in their debts from Chesney, and Faye admits to Adam that she attacked him

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter takes a walk through Dartmoor National Park, one of the last great wildernesses in the UK. On a seven-mile circular walk from the iconic Hay Tor, Julia tries her hand at climbing its towering granite rocks, learns about the Dartmoor Pony with naturalist Nick Baker, and discovers the myths and legends of the moor with musician Seth Lakeman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi confronts Ray with proof of his bribery, Yasmeen suffers a panic attack at work, and Sean encourages Gemma to recruit Vanessa

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: After dealing with the repercussions of her actions from last year, DC Lisa Armstrong is given the opportunity to step up when asked to assist a murder investigation in Morecambe. Tensions remain high as Lisa struggles to do her job whilst following the orders of her previously lower ranking colleague, Med. As the team tries to uncover why the victim was targeted, Lisa and her children are unknowingly stalked by a mysterious figure. Return of the crime drama, starring Morven Christie, Daniel Ryan and Stephen Tompkinson

programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up their Thai 'no fish' cakes and demonstrate how to turn meat-free sausages into smoky chorizo for a piri piri traybake. programme.title: The Chasers Road Trip: Trains, Brains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty, Mark Labbett and Shaun Wallace - three of the biggest brains of the quizzing world - travel the world to uncover the cognitive abilities of animals, children and artificial intelligence. They start out in the USA, where they pit their wits against Kanzi, a bonobo ape who is able to communicate with humans using a series of pictures and symbols - and is also thought to be one of the first apes to show an understanding of spoken English. They also meet a chimpanzee memory wizard, a 25-year-old orang-utan that enjoys playing video games and that well-known genius of the ocean, the dolphin

programme.desc: As the makeover show returns, Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Fareham near Southampton to surprise Betty Richards, a life-long volunteer. programme.desc: Abi confronts Ray with proof of his bribery, Yasmeen suffers a panic attack at work, and Sean encourages Gemma to recruit Vanessa

programme.desc: After dealing with the repercussions of her actions from last year, DC Lisa Armstrong is given the opportunity to step up when asked to assist a murder investigation in Morecambe. Tensions remain high as Lisa struggles to do her job whilst following the orders of her previously lower ranking colleague, Med. As the team tries to uncover why the victim was targeted, Lisa and her children are unknowingly stalked by a mysterious figure. Return of the crime drama, starring Morven Christie, Daniel Ryan and Stephen Tompkinson

Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden

programme.desc: Heather tells her daughters about her relationship with Terry. Chris has to deliver some terrible news to Tara. Marlon is happy about Kathy offering to buy out Eric

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy worries that Eric is up to something, and he attempts to bribe a magistrate. Tara carries on as if nothing has happened, leading Biff to worry about her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley worries that Maxine thinks he is a joke. Fiona and Alan set their wedding date. Kevin succumbs to Sally's demands and tells her he will try to find the money for her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rachel asks Steve out on a date. Fiona tells Jim to stay away from her and Alan. Samantha hears screaming and finds Leanne being attacked by Mickey and Pete

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Called to investigate the attempted theft of a headless skeleton from an archaeological dig, Wycliffe finds himself forced to deal with landowners whose archaic values are jarringly at odds with the modern world. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The rural bliss of Ferne Basset is rudely shattered by the discovery of an elderly resident's body in the river. When Barnaby and Troy descend on the village to ferret out the culprit, they get caught up in simmering backstage rivalries at a local amateur dramatics group that Joyce has recently joined - and the situation grows more sinister when one of the actors is tricked into killing himself in the middle of a performance

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. When Stan is found dead the next day, investigations reveal his demise may not have been through natural causes. Meanwhile, Geoff is baffled by a boy reporting a crime that has yet to happen, and Peggy's conscience starts to trouble her when she hears the lad has gone missing. Guest starring Sue Jenkins

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric tries to talk Alan into taking on his licence. Tara gets upset after visiting her father's house. Lord Michael attempts to ban Biff from attending Tara's father's funeral

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff does not want to be used by Tara just to shock her friends, and Zak plans to steal some clothes for Mandy's shop from the holiday village

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les buys an old banger which he intends to do up and sell on. Mavis gets upset on Derek's birthday. Des encourages Samantha to face her parents and tell them what happened

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha stalls on seeing her parents, but Des promises to go with her. Jim is delighted when Steve goes out with Rachel. Fiona takes Maxine with her to find a wedding dress

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol asks Joe for assistance when suspicions mount against her following the death of a pensioner who she frequently visited. Peggy is annoyed to find a pile of rubbish has been dumped in her backyard and Gina shares her concerns with Carol as she approaches the end of her pregnancy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff and Tara know they are probably the talk of the village. Zak and Butch start a dry cleaning scam at the holiday village. Steve rushes James to hospital

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Steve anxiously wait for news on James at the hospital. Tara has a shock at her father's will reading. Lyn realises that Kirsty is bulimic and offers to get her help

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie is upset when Kevin refuses to let her spend time with his children. Fiona is surprised to see Steve kissing Rachel. Gary worries that Zoe will report him to the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera looks forward to her 40th wedding anniversary. Gary refuses to give Zoe the money up front. Mickey and Pete scare Maud and rob the shop

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish detective's silver wedding celebrations are interrupted by a fresh outbreak of crime on his patch. Five dead bodies have been found on board an abandoned lorry at the ferry terminal and a fugitive is on the run. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Respected surgeon Dan Marsden is shot dead days before his retirement and his teenage daughters Mira and Karen are abducted. Joined by a new addition to the ranks, Vera investigates a mysterious parcel and a connection to the Iraq War as she races against time to catch the killer and save the girls. However, the case soon takes a violent and unexpected turn, striking at the heart of the team. Guest starring Saskia Reeves (Luther) and Dean Andrews (Ashes to Ashes), with Brenda Blethyn, David Leon and Gareth Farr

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Heartbeat
