programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of a crooked car dealer whose son is dying of leukaemia presents McCoy and Ross with a moral dilemma. His ex-wife is their prime suspect - but it transpires that the victim had regularly abused her and refused to pay child support. Drama, starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Green investigate when an adulterous art dealer is stoned to death. The detectives learn that the victim's plan to display provocative art led to unwanted attention from a terrorist organisation and gives them cause to suspect her son - a fanatical Christian - was persuaded by a religious leader to kill his mother. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green comes under investigation by Internal Affairs after he shoots a suspect, and the case reveals a host of secrets the detective would rather keep hidden, threatening not only his police career but his freedom. Jesse L Martin stars, alongside S Epatha Merkerson and Linus Roache

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The officers leap into action when a financial planner is killed in her apartment building. It soon becomes apparent that the culprit could be a hit-man who was intending to murder someone else who happened to resemble the victim. Crime drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of a man visiting New York City to confront his past, but as they delve into the victim's personal life, the case becomes complicated when all clues point to a revered sheriff. Meanwhile, McCoy faces competition when a new district attorney is nominated for election

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of memoir writer Sweetie Ness. Clues initially lead them to suspect his stalker, agent and a groupie, but after digging deeper into the victim's past, they begin to consider the possibility he was not who he claimed to be

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony poses as a convict and takes part in a bogus jailbreak as part of an undercover operation to recover millions of dollars' worth of art and archaeological finds looted from Iraq - but disappears while manacled to a murderous fellow inmate. Guest starring Frank Whaley (Pulp Fiction), with Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A private investigator finds the body of a missing Navy officer in a car at the bottom of a lake and Gibbs reluctantly allows him to assist the investigation into the cause of death. The promise of a $1million reward proves to be a major incentive for the outsider, and the discovery of a bullet leads to suspicions that the man was murdered

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The suspected murder of a petty officer turns out to be a ghoulish practical joke - but a genuine death follows shortly afterwards. As inquiries proceed, local investigators are also assigned to the case - and prove to have personal characteristics remarkably similar to those of Gibbs's staff. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky identifies two dismembered bodies as those of a prosecutor and a sheriff he worked with on a rape case 10 years previously, and realises he could be the killer's next target. Despite Gibbs' attempts to protect him, he is later kidnapped - and the team's search for clues leads to a macabre showdown at a funeral home. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the killing of two contact lens salesmen and a plastic surgeon, who are all murdered during what appear to be botched robberies. The detectives arrest a paranoid schizophrenic for the shootings - but while he is in custody, someone else dies. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A bystander is killed in a robbery, traumatising his mentally ill wife, who is later suspected of physically abusing their child. The police take the youngster into protective custody, but a lack of evidence forces them to return him home - with tragic consequences. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The body of a teenage girl is found in the boot of a car, and inquiries soon establish she was a bully who enjoyed picking on other girls - leaving Stabler and Benson with a seemingly endless list of suspects to choose from. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Benson enlist the help of Dr Huang as they struggle to find a connection between a major pharmaceutical firm's illegal marketing campaign and the fatal shooting of two high-school students. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and BD Wong

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An eminent forensic psychiatrist is found beaten up and left for dead in a drug den. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.desc: A woman's murder leads Lupo and Bernard to learn she was secretly in conflict with her husband, going against his desire that their family lead an environmentally friendly life. Guest starring Ned Beatty (Deliverance) and Sherry Stringfield (ER)

programme.desc: Gibbs and the team try to find out who is responsible for sending a parcel containing a pair of blue human eyeballs to the home of a petty officer who works in Intelligence. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.desc: The rape and murder of a criminology student puts detectives Stabler and Benson on the trail of a killer with an expert knowledge of forensics. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: Stabler tries to rescue a youngster who has been kidnapped by a man with schizophrenia, but resorts to unorthodox tactics that jeopardise his own career. programme.desc: Antiques experts Paul Laidlaw and Claire Rawle set off on the final road trip leg from the Derbyshire town of Matlock. Paul also unravels the story behind the world-famous crooked church tower at Chesterfield and Claire meets a relative of the first man to circumnavigate Australia, who hails from the Lincolnshire town of Donnington. The pressure is on at the final auction in Stamford, Lincolnshire.

programme.title: My Violent Child
programme.desc: Therapist Mandy Saligari heads to Nottingham, where single mum Hazel has six children but is struggling to deal with one of them, who is out of control and showing signs of violence. Eight-year old Jordan rules the roost, terrorising his five siblings, punching his mum, and trashing the furniture, so Mandy uses a blend of plain speaking and direct action to help him and his family

programme.title: Embarrassing Fat Bodies
programme.desc: Pixie McKenna encounters three huge hernias, and Christian Jessen meets Malissa Jones, who, aged 18, became one of the youngest patients in Britain to undergo an operation to fit a gastric band in 2008

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard and Tee John take a road trip in Hungary. In Budapest, they visit one of the country's biggest antique markets. Later, they unearth treasures in a former communist factory

programme.title: Ghost Adventures
programme.desc: Ghost hunters Zak Bagans, Nick Groff and Aaron Goodwin visit one of the largest prisons in Pennsylvania in search of the spirits of former convicts

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and her team are called to grade II listed building Middleton Hall, Staffordshire, which dates back over 900 years. Much paranormal activity has been reported there, and Yvette soon sees something that shocks her to the core

programme.desc: Hull is the starting point for Paul Laidlaw and Claire Rawle as they search the north east of England, picking up a rare 18th-century puzzle jug and some Shelley porcelain

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Paul Laidlaw and Claire Rawle set off on the final road trip leg from the Derbyshire town of Matlock. Militaria mad Paul hopes a late 17th-century hand-forged halberd head earns him a huge profit. Claire buys a Chinese cloisonné vase and an Edwardian inkstand. Paul also unravels the story behind the world-famous crooked church tower at Chesterfield and Claire meets a relative of the first man to circumnavigate Australia, who hails from the Lincolnshire town of Donnington. The pressure is on at the final auction in Stamford, Lincolnshire.

programme.desc: Two athletes, hurdler Colin Jackson and triple jumper Jonathan Edwards, take part in the challenge, searching for antiques in Greater Manchester, Cheshire and Nottinghamshire before heading to auction in Bedford

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore a flat in Cornwall, a house in Co Durham and a property in Chiswick, west London. They then follow each of the sales at auction and talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra builds a factory when McMeer's runs out of coconut cake

programme.desc: The tales of Zot, a good-hearted, fun-loving but rather bouncy dog and his best pal, Clive

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: The wacky adventures of the cookie-loving dragons who live in a wizard's castle

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Lee, Bermondsey and Stratford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien may have ulterior motives for inviting Tracey to a posh event, while Sharon makes a tidy profit out of fake Rolling Stones T-shirts. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens, which house a diverse collection of plants and are a leading centre for scientific research and conservation

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: A bomb is found in a nightclub which German scientists claim cannot be disarmed, and Ash receives a mysterious message. Guest starring Judy Geeson (the original 1970s Poldark)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time (1963)
programme.desc: A butcher's bumbling assistant causes no end of accidents at work, the latest of which has landed his boss in hospital. While visiting, he meets a little girl who has not spoken since her parents died, and resolves to put a smile on the orphan's face. Slapstick comedy, starring Norman Wisdom, Edward Chapman, Jerry Desmonde, Lucy Appleby and Jill Melford

programme.title: Paper Tiger (1975)
programme.desc: An English tutor enthralls a Japanese ambassador's son with improbable fantasies of his heroic exploits, but then finds himself forced to act them out for real when he and the boy are both kidnapped. Comedy adventure, starring David Niven, Toshiro Mifune, Hardy Kruger and Ivan Desny

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 36-year-old man with gout visits the hospital, and despite the ailment's age-old associations with elderly men and rich living, he discovers his is hereditary. The doctors hear from a woman seeking a dietary cure for depression, and a mother-of-three who has lived on pancakes and chocolate for two years. Dr Pixie McKenna is also on hand to reveal the truth about detox diets

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef joins an unusual supper club that meets on an old Tube carriage, and makes sausage-and-mash for some taxi drivers

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Otway: The Movie (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about the punk singer, following his career from the 1970s to the present day and offering his insights into how to survive in the music business

programme.title: Blue Iguana (2018)
programme.desc: Eddie and Paul, former prisoners working in a New York diner, are made an offer they cannot refuse by English lawyer Katherine Rookwood. Romantic comedy, starring Sam Rockwell, Phoebe Fox and Ben Schwartz

programme.title: The Weekend (2016)
programme.desc: Three friends stumble across £100,000, and spend it all on a shopping spree and a wild party. However, thwey then discover that the money belongs to a feared gangster - and he expects them to pay back every penny. Comedy, starring Joivan Wade, Percelle Ascott and Dee Kaate

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population

programme.title: Rag Doll (1961)
programme.desc: A teenager runs away from her alcoholic stepfather to start a new life in London, only to fall under the influence of a seductive criminal, and get drawn into helping him rob her kindly boss. Crime drama, starring Christina Gregg, Jess Conrad and Hermione Baddeley

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Lee, Bermondsey and Stratford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks along the Isle of Man's stunning coast and visits Sprotbrough in South Yorkshire

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Laura buys a new dress for a special dinner with Ben. However, when her date reveals he is married, Mike happens by to pick up the pieces with his usual charm. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's second Vengeance weapon, the deadly V2 rocket, which was responsible for killing 3,000 people in London and the south of England during six terrifying months

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash gets some time off and plans to spend it in bed - but Susan has other ideas

programme.title: The Upturned Glass (1947)
programme.desc: A surgeon tells a class of students the story of his tragic affair with the married mother of a patient. When she later died under suspicious circumstances, he became convinced she was killed by her jealous sister-in-law, and decided to avenge her death by committing a murder of his own. Drama, starring James Mason, Pamela Kellino and Rosamund John

programme.title: The Chiltern Hundreds (1949)
programme.desc: An aristocrat fails to be elected as a Tory MP, so decides to stand for Labour instead _ only to learn his butler, determined to maintain class distinctions, is running against him. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, AE Matthews and David Tomlinson

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef joins an unusual supper club that meets on an old Tube carriage, and makes sausage-and-mash for some taxi drivers

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: Shaw Somers, Lucy Jones and Dr Giovanni Miletto give advice to a 42-year-old woman who has been eliminating entire food groups from her diet in an effort to combat her traumatising fish odour syndrome. They also help a teenager who worries severe eczema will prevent him fulfilling his dream of becoming an aeroplane cabin crew member. Three hard-drinking students from Leeds put hangover cures to the test, and Dr Pixie McKenna investigates the veracity of health claims made on food packaging

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s. This edition focuses on the story of glam rock. Featuring David Bowie, Alice Cooper, Jethro Tull and Elton John

programme.title: Budding Business
programme.desc: Millions are being made Australia's medicinal marijuana business and entrepreneurs are riding the 'pot stock' boom. But with patients struggling to access cannabis products who is it benefitting?

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: A schoolteacher in a quiet village in Norfolk is torn between life with her boring husband and embarking on an affair with a pop star. Dawn French and Jennifer Saunders star

programme.title: Hand to Mouth
programme.desc: A working class slice of life comedy, about life, love and family on a budget

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: National Treasure
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane stars as Paul Finchley, one half of a much-loved comedy double act, whose life is turned upside down when he is arrested after an accusation of rape that dates back to the 1990s. Paul adamantly denies having even met the woman making the allegation and his loyal wife (Julie Walters) must decide whether to stand by him, while his troubled daughter (Andrea Riseborough) begins to question her relationship with her father

programme.title: Scramble (2017)
programme.desc: A man ends up in over his head in the world of organised crime when his girlfriend is kidnapped by an underground prostitution ring. His attempts to raise the ransom money land him a job delivering dead bodies for a crime syndicate. Crime thriller, starring Brett Newton and Victor Kelso

programme.title: Dagenham ()
programme.desc: A mother's fight against drugs, death and desperation in the deprived town of Dagenham

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: Jamie infiltrates a band of anti-capitalist terrorists who are using a state-of-the-art sniper's rifle, but his MI5 contact in the group loses her nerve and reveals his identity during preparations for their next hit. As Box 500 races to crack a coded message giving his location, Henno decides to take matters into his own hands and Red Troop embarks on a tense rescue bid. Drama, starring Jamie Draven and Ross Kemp

programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring and revealing Shadwell and Grove Park for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

The results of the first Big Food Trial are announced, and Pixie McKenna investigates the reliability of do-it-yourself food-intolerance tests

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A cross-border collie suffers a setback when a disc from its spine blows and Noel Fitzpatrick designs a special bolt to substitute it. A four-year-old cat is taken to the surgery after being hit by a car, and it is bad news for the owners when the X-rays reveal its pelvis is badly damaged and the operation may cost thousands of pounds

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the fate of the 'Dunlop' family of foxes in Essex as they fight for survival in the wild. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Comedy series, starring Roger Lloyd Pack and Clive Swift, about two lively pensioners who see their age as no barrier to having fun - particularly when it comes to their attractive neighbour, played by Jane Asher. Written by Sam Bain and Jesse Armstrong, creators of Channel 4's hit sitcom Peep Show, and Simon Blackwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Weekend (2016)
programme.desc: Three friends stumble across £100,000, and spend it all on a shopping spree and a wild party. However, thwey then discover that the money belongs to a feared gangster - and he expects them to pay back every penny. Comedy, starring Joivan Wade, Percelle Ascott and Dee Kaate

programme.title: Priest of Love (1981)
programme.desc: Christopher Miles's dramatisation of author DH Lawrence's years in the US, Mexico and England, charting events following the banning of his novel The Rainbow to the writing of Lady Chatterley's Lover. Starring Ian McKellen, Janet Suzman, Ava Gardner, Penelope Keith and John Gielgud

programme.title: Otway: The Movie (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about the punk singer, following his career from the 1970s to the present day and offering his insights into how to survive in the music business

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Notting Hill for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Date with a Dream (1948)
programme.desc: Four wartime performers well known for their rousing concert recitals decide to reform and become a successful nightclub act all over again. Upbeat comedy, starring Wally Patch, Len Lowe, Terry-Thomas, Norman Wisdom and Jean Carson

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series presented by Andrew White, featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain

programme.title: Fight Night International
programme.desc: Carl Froch v Andre Ward. Another chance to see the Super Six final at the Boardwalk Hall in Atlantic City, New Jersey, where the WBC and WBA Super Middleweight titles were also on the line. Nottingham's Froch bounced back from his sole career loss on points to Mikkel Kessler to reach this final by winning decisions against Arthur Abraham and Glen Johnson. However, the Cobra faced a difficult task against the American, who was unbeaten in his 24 professional bouts and won light heavyweight gold at the 2004 Olympics

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights of the third day of the Third Test at Old Trafford, where the series passed the halfway point. England have won the last two Test series between the nations and made the best possible start to this one, with a dramatic 14-run victory at Trent Bridge, followed by an emphatic 347-run win at Lord's. Alastair Cook's men only need a draw to retain the Ashes, while Australia have not claimed the famous urn on English soil since 2001

programme.desc: Liverpool v Burnley (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight match from Anfield, as Liverpool continue their quest to retain their top-flight title for the first time since the 1982-83 season.
